evidence.quote: We extracted all the functionally annotated proteins from the DisProt9.3 database.
We removed sequences whose functional regions lacked annotations for disordered structure. We also excluded the DP00072 sequence that was too long (>30,000 residues)
Subsequently, a total of 925 sequences were obtained
clustered the 925 sequences using the CD-HIT algorithm [60]

with 25% sequence similarity. Then, we randomly divided the clusters into five subsets, where three subsets (including 589 sequences) were used as the training dataset, and one subset (including 148 sequences) was used as the validation dataset. The remaining subset with 188 sequences was used as the independent test dataset, namely DP93.
embeddings extracted from the ProtT5 [64] protein language model
the model using ProtT5 consistently achieved the highest performance in predicting disorder and disordered functions.

a bidirectional gated recurrent neural network (Bi-GRU) [66] layer is employed... GRU layer with an attention mechanism [19, 67]... six fully connected layers were employed for mapping... graph convolutional network (GCN) layer was used
used the IG matrix pre-calculated on the training dataset... as the initialization value
employed the binary cross-entropy loss function... All the model variables and hyper-parameters were optimized according to the minimum loss function values on the validation dataset.
evaluated the predictive performances... with threshold-independent metrics... AUC... AUPR... APS... and F_max... MCC and balanced accuracy (BACC)
performance of DisoFLAG in predicting disordered protein-binding, DNA-binding, RNA-binding, lipid-binding, and linkers is better than the current tools

step_scores.label: mostly_supported

evidence.quote: additional independent test dataset (DP94) containing 98 sequences... collected from the newly updated proteins in versions 9.3 to 9.4
performance... is still significantly better... in predicting disordered protein-binding, DNA-binding, and linkers; however, its performance decreased in predicting RNA-binding and lipid-bindings

issues.reason: Step provided empty references list, though text mentions 'Additional file 1: Tables S7 and S8' for DP94 results. Traceability is reduced.

step_scores.fix_suggestion: Add reference to 'Additional file 1: Tables S7 and S8' or relevant text section in the references field.

evidence.quote: disorder-binding dataset contains 78 proteins... disorder-linker dataset contains 40 proteins
DisoFLAG's protein-binding predictor generates the highest quality predictions with AUC = 0.879 and APS = 0.563... linker predictor achieves AUC = 0.8
Disorder-NOX (210 proteins) and Disorder-PDB (348 proteins)
DisoFLAG achieved a second rank with an AUC of 0.836... on the Disorder-NOX dataset.
used layer-wise relevance propagation (LRP) [81, 82]
These correlations consistently made a positive contribution to the prediction of six disordered functions
human immunodeficiency virus infectivity factor (HIV-1 Vif, DisProt: DP00875)... threshold that achieves the maximum F1 score

DisoFLAG is the only method that can simultaneously identify the complete disordered protein-binding and lipid-binding regions of the Vif protein and has the lowest number of false-positive predictions.
